lessonKeywords.description: The amount of rotation between two rays.
A reliable way to classify an angle as acute, right, or obtuse.

misconceptionsAndCommonMistakes.misconception: Pupils may think an obtuse angle is any angle that looks large.
misconceptionsAndCommonMistakes.response: Compare each angle to a right angle rather than relying on first impressions.

lessonKeywords.description: The given line on which the starting point lies.
At the same distance from a chosen point.

misconceptionsAndCommonMistakes.misconception: Pupils may place the perpendicular by eye rather than by construction.
misconceptionsAndCommonMistakes.response: Use equal marks and a justified construction so the right angle follows from the geometry.

lessonKeywords.description: An angle formed when one side of a polygon is extended.

misconceptionsAndCommonMistakes.misconception: Pupils may think the angle sum changes when the triangle changes shape.
misconceptionsAndCommonMistakes.response: Vary the triangle and keep tracking the three interior angles back to a straight angle.
